segments.text: Job replied,
You really think you're special people, don't you? Obviously when you die, wisdom will die with you!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But I too have insights, and you're no better than me. Doesn't everyone know the things you've said?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But my friends laugh at me because I called on God and he answered me: the innocent man who does right has become an object of derision.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: People who are comfortable have contempt for those who are in trouble, ready to push over those who are already slipping.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Robbers live in peace, and those who make God angry live in safety, trusting their own strength as their ‘god.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if you ask the animals they will teach you, the birds in the sky will tell you;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ask the earth and it will teach you, and the fishes in the sea will tell you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Which of all these doesn't know that the Lord has done this?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He gives life to every living thing, life to all humankind.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The ear distinguishes words just like the palate distinguishes foods.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Wisdom to distinguish belongs to the old, and the ability to rightly discriminate belongs to those with long experience.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God has wisdom and power, counsel and understanding belong to him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If he tears something down, nobody can rebuild it. If he imprisons someone, nobody can free them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If God holds back the waters, everything dries up; if he releases the waters, the earth floods.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He is mighty and victorious;  both deceivers and those deceived are subject to him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He leads counselors away stripped of their wisdom, he makes judges into fools.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He removes the chains of office from kings and makes them wear loincloths.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He leads priests away stripped of their religious garments, he overthrows the powerful.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He takes away the advice of trusted advisors, he removes the discernment of the elders.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He pours scorn upon princes and takes away power from the strong.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He reveals what is hidden in darkness, and brings into the light the shadow of death.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He makes nations great and he destroys them; he expands nations and ruins them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He removes the understanding of rulers and makes them wander in the wilderness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They grope around in the dark without a light. He makes them stagger like drunk people.
segments.speaker: Unknown
